sglang.title: [Refactor] Rename NSA → DSA: user-facing aliases, file/class/import rename
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
NSA (Native Sparse Attention) is a misnomer — this attention variant is specific to DeepSeek and should be called DSA (DeepSeek Sparse Attention). This PR performs a full rename from nsa to dsa across user-facing CLI/env/registry surfaces and internal files, while preserving backward-comp

sglang.title: ci(sgl-router): add PR test workflow (pre-positioned for feature PR)
sglang.author: Kangyan-Zhou
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Lands the sgl-router PR-test workflow on main ahead of the feature PR (#25851). The workflow is path-filtered to experimental/sgl-router/** and scripts/ci/cuda/ci_install_dependency.sh, so until either path is touched by a PR, this file is dormant on main — no jobs run, no resources consumed

During DeepSeek V4 prefill with chunked_prefill_size=8192, the c4 attention layers (30 out of 61 layers, total_topk=640) previously required allocating a ~5 GB intermediate gathered_kv buffer for the separate gather+attention path. This exceeded the 2 GB chunking limit, forcing 3-chunk p

sglang.title: [Bug][Mamba] Fix LRU list reference cycle leak in mamba_radix_cache
sglang.author: zjd0112
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fix memory leak in LRUList._remove_node that causes scheduler RSS to grow linearly under sustained load on hybrid Mamba models (e.g., Qwen3-Next).
In our test (Qwen3-Next on RTX 5090 TP=2), RSS grows at ~3.7 GB/h.
Related: #21535
Root cause
LRUList._remove_node updates the neighbors to sk

bench_serving --disable-stream crashed for reasoning models because the non-streaming OpenAI chat path copied only choices[0].message.content into RequestFuncOutput.generated_text. With separate_reasoning enabled, the server can split generated tokens across message.reasoning

sglang.title: [DSA] Make MQA logits free memory ratio configurable
sglang.author: YAMY1234
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The cached DSA MQA logits budget can be too loose on memory-tight H200 runs. Make the free-memory fraction configurable and use a more conservative default so the logits path chunks earlier instead of relying on a stale cached budget.
Modifications

Rebase on the NSA to DSA rename and mov

sglang.title: [RL][TITO] Preserve whitespace in reasoning parser outputs
sglang.author: zyzshishui
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Under the TITO path in RL, we want to preserve model-generated messages as much as it originally is, which means we should avoid doing modifications to whatever the model generates.
This issue was found while validating token-in-token-out (TITO) support for DeepSeek-V4-Flash. During testi

python/sglang/jit_kernel/fp8_quantize.py (new) — fp8 e4m3 quanti

--load-format dummy only initializes floating-point tensors, leaving HashTopK.tid2eid uninitialized because it is an integer lookup table. For DeepSeek-V4-Flash with flashinfer_mxfp4, this can produce invalid expert ids and trigger CUDA illegal memory accesses during hash to

sglang.title: [SMG] Support regular worker discovery alongside PD workers in IGW mode
sglang.author: Gruner-atero
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When running with --pd-disaggregation, service discovery only accepts pods matching prefill_selector or decode_selector. Pods matching --selector (regular workers) are silently dropped, making it impossible to serve multiple models (some via PD, some via regular routing) from a single rou

sglang.title: Address overlap future token map by request-pool index
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Why req-pool-indexed
The ring allocator slot id is a temporary counter — slot K denotes different requests at different times, driving sizing miscalculations (chunked-prefill blowup), wrap-around aliasing, slot-reuse staleness, and a cross-stream indices-tensor lifetime race. Indexing every future-m

sglang.title: [Fix] DSV4 cached_loc invalidated when SWA mapping is rebuilt

sglang.bodyExcerpt:

When `SGLANG_OPT_CACHE_SWA_TRANSLATION=True`, `DeepSeekV4TokenToKVPool` caches the full→SWA index translation in `self.cached_loc` to avoid redundant GPU gathers. However, `register_mapping()` replaces `full_to_swa_index_mapping` without clearing `cached_loc`, so subsequent translations c

This PR MUST be merged after this change flashinfer-ai/flashinfer#3155 is merged in Flashinfer and vLLM starts to use this FI version. There is a bug in GDN implementation in FI.

vllm.title: [Feature] Support manually enabling the cumem allocator
vllm.author: kebe7jun
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Follow up: #33540 (comment)
Adding the --enable-cumem-allocator parameter allows users to manually enable the cumem allocator without sleep mode, which is important when using Nixl for PD isolation in the GB series.
I have run tests on GB200 and H200, and both are working properly.
Test Plan

Adds specdec support for NemotronH: EAGLE3, P-EAGLE, DFlash.
Also allows for EAGLE-based speculators to use a number of aux features other than 3 or 0.

Works locally. Models not yet released.

With structural tags, a reasoning parser, and speculative decoding, we sometimes get logits that are not grammar-masked (allow-all bitmask), which leads to unconstrained token generation when we expect guided decoding.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Use enable_sm120_family for per-tensor FP8 CUTLASS kernels on SM12.1

Purpose
Fix per-tensor FP8 CUTLASS kernels failing on SM 12.1 devices (e.g., NVIDIA GB10).
The cutlass_3x_gemm_sm120 and cutlass_3x_gemm_sm120_custom structs use enable_sm120_only which guards on __CUDA_ARCH == 1200. On SM 12.1 (__CUDA_ARCH__ == 1210), this causes a kernel trap and surfaces as cutla

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix Qwen3.5 GatedDeltaNet in_proj_ba Marlin failure at TP>=2
vllm.author: sonusflow
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fixes #35924 — Qwen3.5 GatedDeltaNet in_proj_ba fails Marlin MIN_THREAD_N=64 at TP>=2.
Root cause: in_proj_ba uses MergedColumnParallelLinear with output_sizes=[num_v_heads] * 2. When TP-sharded, the per-rank output falls below Marlin's minimum (e.g., 397B: 64 / TP=4 = 16 < 64).
Fix: Replace

processing images/frames serially through the vision tower.
The baseline code calls the full vision tower (vt(pv, pp, output_length))
once per image or video frame in a Python loop. Under concurrent serving load,
this se
